sections.paragraph:
τόνος, ου, ὁ (τείνω ‘stretch’; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Philo; Jos., Bell. 6, 162) prim. ‘stretching, tension’, in our lit. only fig. force, lasting quality (Plut., Brut. 999 [34, 3] τῆς ὀργῆς) ὁ φόβος αὐτοῦ (the devil) τόνον οὐκ ἔχει=fear of the devil puts one under no strain Hm 12, 4, 7.—DELG s.v. τανυ-E.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

prim. prim. = primarily

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1010
